hypothesis: After the accepted Technology and Commodity trend target-width repairs, another trend sector may still be losing EV because winners are clipped by the shared 4.5 ATR target. Widening only one remaining trend sector at a time should identify whether the winner-truncation mechanism generalizes beyond those two accepted sleeves.

best_positive_but_one_window_only.decision_note: Rejected for production because the improvement had qualifying impact only in old_thin and zero impact in the other two fixed windows.

second_best_one_window_only.decision_note: Rejected for the same one-window-only exposure problem.

damaging_families.Financials: All 5.0/6.0/7.0 ATR variants damaged both mid_weak and old_thin EV.
damaging_families.Energy: All 5.0/6.0/7.0 ATR variants damaged late_strong EV by -0.2366.
damaging_families.Healthcare: All tested variants were strict null across the fixed windows.

llm_metrics.blocker_relation: LLM soft-ranking remains blocked by candidate-level replay coverage, so this run deliberately used deterministic exit alpha instead.

rejection_reason: No remaining sector-specific trend target-width variant improved a majority of non-overlapping windows. The only positive candidates were one-window old_thin effects; other sectors were null or damaging.
next_retry_requires: Do not generalize Technology/Commodity trend target widening to additional sectors by default.
Do not promote Consumer Discretionary or Communication Services trend target widening until another non-overlapping or forward sample has qualifying exposure.
Do not retry Financials or Energy trend target widening by only nudging 5-7 ATR values.
Future exit-alpha work needs a fresh winner-truncation audit with exposure in at least two windows, or a new trade-specific hold-quality signal.
verification: Runtime-only screen on fixed OHLCV snapshots completed enough to print the full summary; command exceeded the tool timeout after producing the result summary.
No production code was changed, so no pytest run was required for this rejected screen.
notes: This was alpha_search. It changed no production code and did not treat LLM as the problem. It directly tests and rejects the simple sector-by-sector extrapolation of the accepted Technology and Commodity trend target-width mechanism.
